title: Discontent with Putin rises among part of the Russian population: 'There is an immense wall between you and us'
subhead: Internet shutdowns, a struggling economy and the ongoing war in Ukraine have exasperated some Russians, while a recent poll shows the president's popularity is slipping.

Middle East crisis could cost France 'at least €6 billion,' says prime minister
subhead: In a letter to his ministers, Sébastien Lecornu detailed the budget cuts required to meet the public deficit target for 2026: €4 billion from the state and €2 billion from social security.

title: Israeli films caught between calls for boycott and government threats
subhead: Their access to international festivals and co-productions has become increasingly complicated as Israel's image has deteriorated since the war of destruction in Gaza that followed the October 7 terrorist attack. The culture minister, for his part, is threatening to cut off state funding.

title: UNESCO sites are havens for rare biodiversity under growing threat
subhead: The first global assessment of these 2,260 listed sites, which cover more than 13 million square kilometers, reveals that they serve as sanctuaries for wildlife species.

title: Former French detainee in Qatar condemns Elysée's 'silence' and Macron's 'inaction'
subhead: André, a French cybersecurity expert, was imprisoned in Qatar for 264 days for computer data theft. In an interview with Le Monde, he denounced the 'biased' judicial system, his detention conditions and Emmanuel Macron's 'inaction.'

title: From a South American frog to a Russian penal colony: How the poison epibatidine killed Navalny
subhead: This extremely powerful neurotoxin is secreted by a frog native to Ecuador. Although Western scientists discovered it in the 19th century, it wasn't synthesized until the 1990s. The neurotoxin appears to have particularly interested the Russian research institute responsible for synthesizing Novichok agents, used in poisonings.
byline: Audrey Lagadec,  Eric Dedier and  Rémi Ducasse

title: France's business elite warms to the far right
subhead: Leaders of France's largest companies are no longer shying away from meetings with the Rassemblement National's leaders, as they seek to influence the economic agenda of one of the leading contenders for the 2027 presidential election.
